title: Revision Selection, Ranges, Search, and Change Discovery · Conflict Triage
summary: Answer a focused history question without checking out candidate commits or mistaking range notation for set subtraction in every context. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a revision-query and change-discovery workbook and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debu…
objectives: Resolve full and abbreviated object IDs, refs, reflog selectors, ancestry suffixes, and peel operators with ambiguity checks.
Interpret two-dot, three-dot, negative revisions, symmetric difference, and command-specific diff behavior.
Use pathspec magic, exclusions, attributes, top-level anchoring, and separator placement without confusing paths with revisions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Order the regression proof
steps.instruction: Order the Change Review Ranges evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Change Review Ranges assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Feature tip F has 3 commits after merge base M while main has two. Compare M..F, main...F, HEAD~2, and path-limited pickaxe results without confusing commit sets with file diffs. Competency probe: Choose a review range that matches branch intent, merge base, stacked work, and force-push risk.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Change Review Ranges has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Change Review Ranges precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Feature tip F has 3 commits after merge base M while main has two. Compare M..F, main...F, HEAD~2, and path-limited pickaxe results without confusing commit sets with file diffs. Competency probe: Choose a review range that matches branch intent, merge base, stacked work, and force-push risk.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Comparing two branch tips always isolates only the proposed work.

options.text: Inspect repository boundary, config origin, current operation, worktree, index, refs, and graph for the debug Change Review Ranges case
Preserve uncommitted work and a recovery ref, then state intended tree and topology for the debug Change Review Ranges case
Preview and perform the least destructive simulated Git operation for the debug Change Review Ranges case
Verify status, staged and range diffs, graph, tests, remote target, and recovery path for the debug Change Review Ranges case

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Resolve full and abbreviated object IDs, refs, reflog selectors, ancestry suffixes, and peel operators with ambiguity checks. Artifact under review: a revision-query and change-discovery workbook.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Revision Names, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Revision Names record says a revision-query and change-discovery workbook looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. This does not resolve inspect the failure record.

For inspect the failure record, apply Revision Names without its stated preconditions, omit the a revision-query and change-discovery workbook trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Revision Names for a revision-query and change-discovery workbook.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a safe repository investigation headed "# Evidence: git-change-review-ranges-mcc2qz" with a concrete hypothesis, one bounded simulator command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Change Review Ranges into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: git-change-review-ranges-mcc2qz
# hypothesis: [replace with one falsifiable cause]
# command: choose one bounded simulator observation
# observation: [replace with the result that changes the next decision]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Change Review Ranges structural check is only one gate. a revision-query and change-discovery workbook must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Choose a review range that matches branch intent, merge base, stacked work, and force-push risk.
